signals.reasoning: Cover the live short immediately. The position is already losing money, retained none of its tiny peak PnL, has no stop, is flagged urgent, and liquidation distance is only about 0.97%, which is far too tight to defend under an active geopolitical veto.
BTC remains in weak post-flush stabilization, but there is no clean fresh trigger on either side. With the event veto still active, staying flat is higher quality than forcing a new view.
ETH still shows some fresh-shorts-pressing mechanics, but the actual tape is just choppy stabilization rather than clean continuation. That makes it watch-only under current conditions.
SOL has some residual squeeze fuel after the earlier long flush, but it is not giving a trustworthy reclaim signal. Under veto, this remains a watch name rather than an active position.
STABLE still has event-sensitive squeeze characteristics, but the move is already extended and not something to chase in a veto regime. It is better treated as watch-only.
GOAT is already too damaged and too far into flush territory to justify a fresh short, while the long side remains too unstable. There is no clean trade here.
AAVE has decent liquidity and a real bounce, but it still looks more like recovery inside a fragile market than a clean continuation entry. Under active veto, I would not force a new position.
BLUR is weak, but most of the damage is already done and the current structure looks more like late shorting than fresh edge. It stays on the watchlist, not the trade list.
GRASS has bounced sharply, but the combination of moderate liquidity and active event risk keeps it below the bar for a new signal. This is watch-only.
RESOLV has squeeze fuel and a mild bullish mechanical lean, but the chart still looks too fragile and the board is still vetoed. That makes it an observation name, not a deployment name.
0G is still the strongest raw squeeze name on the sheet, but it remains extremely extended, thin, and reversal-prone. In a veto environment, that is not something to chase with fresh capital.
